entries.title: Slow Test
entries.description: Tests exceed expected runtime budget and slow feedback loops.
entries.remediation: Profile slow paths and split or optimize expensive tests.

entries.title: Flaky Test
entries.description: Tests exhibit inconsistent pass/fail behavior across runs.
entries.remediation: Stabilize timing, shared state, and external dependency handling.
entries.promotionPlan: Today's detector is retry-based, not statistical failure-rate. Statistical detection lands in a future release with the calibration corpus.

entries.title: Skipped Test
entries.description: Tests are skipped and may hide latent regressions.
entries.remediation: Unskip, remove, or explicitly justify skipped tests in policy.

entries.title: Dead Test
entries.description: Tests may no longer validate meaningful behavior.
entries.remediation: Remove obsolete tests or reconnect them to active behavior.

entries.title: Unstable Suite
entries.description: The suite has concentrated instability signals.
entries.remediation: Prioritize stabilization in the highest-instability areas.

entries.title: Untested Export
entries.description: Exported code units are not directly covered by tests.
entries.remediation: Add direct tests for public exports to protect API behavior.

entries.title: Weak Assertion
entries.description: Tests use weak or low-density assertions, reducing defect-catching power.
entries.remediation: Add behavior-focused assertions on outputs, state transitions, and side effects.
entries.promotionPlan: Detector is regex/density-based; AST-based semantic scoring lands in a future release alongside the calibration corpus. Gate-tier promotion requires explicit policy threshold (e.g., user-declared strict-vs-pragmatic mode) AND framing-test flip <15%.

entries.title: Mock-Heavy Test
entries.description: Tests rely heavily on mocks and may miss integration-level regressions.
entries.remediation: Replace brittle mocks with real collaborators where practical.
entries.promotionPlan: Underlying hypothesis empirically refuted (corpus lift 0.02x). Framing-instability confirmed. Rebuild requires a mock-classifier distinguishing module vs callback mocks. Defer or remove.

entries.title: Tests Only Mocks
entries.description: Test files contain mock setup but zero assertions, verifying wiring only.
entries.remediation: Add assertions on outputs, state changes, or side effects to validate real behavior.
entries.promotionPlan: Rebuild as repo-aggregate posture metric. Per-file detection blocked by assertion-counter blindness (caught only bare `assert`); A1 multi-dialect oracle would lift to ~55-70% but TP base rate is fundamental ceiling.

entries.title: Snapshot-Heavy Test
entries.description: Test files over-rely on snapshot assertions, reducing defect specificity.
entries.remediation: Supplement snapshots with targeted assertions on critical behavior.
entries.promotionPlan: Gate-tier promotion gated on: (1) n=200+ from ≥40 unique repos; (2) framing flip <15% under threshold-conjunction (snap≥2 AND ratio≥0.3); (3) explicit user-facing policy declaration.

entries.title: Coverage Blind Spot
entries.description: Code units appear unprotected or weakly protected by current coverage mix.
entries.remediation: Add unit/integration tests where only broad or indirect coverage exists.

entries.title: Coverage Threshold Break
entries.description: Measured coverage falls below configured thresholds.
entries.remediation: Target low-coverage, high-risk areas and raise meaningful coverage first.
entries.promotionPlan: Severity flips at hard 100%-gap boundary; a smooth gradient lands in a future release per docs/scoring-rubric.md.

entries.title: Static Skipped Test
entries.description: Tests are statically marked as skipped (it.skip, xit, @skip, etc.).
entries.remediation: Re-enable, replace, or document skip markers older than the policy threshold.

entries.title: Static Skipped Test — Unconditional
entries.description: A test is statically marked as skipped without any surrounding environment / feature-flag gate. The skip is permanent until the marker is removed.
entries.remediation: Re-enable, replace, or delete the test. Add a comment explaining why if the skip should persist.
entries.promotionPlan: Ships behind the static_skipped_test_split mechanism in shadow mode. Promotes to stable when the split's per-mechanism recall + frozen-suite gates clear.

entries.title: Static Skipped Test — Conditional Gate
entries.description: A test is statically marked as skipped, but the skip is wrapped by an environment, feature-flag, or platform predicate. The skip is intentional and gated.
entries.remediation: No remediation required when the gate is correct. Audit the gate periodically; CI should run the test on platforms where the gate is false.
entries.promotionPlan: Ships behind the static_skipped_test_split mechanism in shadow mode. Preserves the 39% of staticSkippedTest TPs that an A3 narrowing would otherwise drop.

entries.title: Assertion-Free Test
entries.description: Test files contain test function signatures but no detectable assertions.
entries.remediation: Add assertions to validate behavior — tests without assertions verify nothing.
entries.promotionPlan: Gate-tier requires: (1) regex-floor lift to 70%+ precision, (2) A3 path-role gate to exclude conftest/fixtures/commented-out, (3) framing test flip <15%.

entries.title: Orphaned Test File
entries.description: Test files do not import any source modules from the repository.
entries.remediation: Connect orphaned tests to source code or remove if obsolete.

entries.title: Dependency Drift Risk
entries.description: A dependency manifest has a high share of moving-target version specs (caret, tilde, *, latest), making the repo silently susceptible to upstream regressions.
entries.remediation: Pin versions or add a lockfile-verification gate. Re-audit the manifest after pinning to confirm the moving-target share drops below the threshold.
entries.promotionPlan: Promotes to stable once the calibration corpus confirms regression-PR lift ≥ 1.5x on deps-bump PRs.

entries.title: Dependency Drift — Strict-Pin
entries.description: Dependencies are declared without an explicit version anchor (bare name, `*`, `latest`, or unversioned URL). The resolver picks whatever happens to be available at install time.
entries.remediation: Add an explicit version, version range, or lockfile-verification gate so installs are reproducible.
entries.promotionPlan: Ships behind the deps_drift_risk_split mechanism in shadow mode. Closes one half of the npm-vs-Poetry-vs-Cargo caret-semantics inconsistency.

entries.title: Dependency Drift — Caret Policy
entries.description: Dependencies use caret-range specs (`^x.y.z`) under an ecosystem whose caret semantics make minor-version drift opaque (npm vs Poetry vs Cargo treat caret differently).
entries.remediation: Adopt a stricter pinning policy (tilde, exact, or commit-pinned) where minor-version drift would silently affect runtime behavior.
entries.promotionPlan: Ships behind the deps_drift_risk_split mechanism in shadow mode. Closes the other half of the caret-semantics inconsistency.

entries.title: Config Schema Drift Risk
entries.description: An infra-config file (GitHub Actions workflow, docker-compose, Helm values, or k8s manifest) uses forward-compat hazards: mutable action refs, `:latest` or untagged image tags, deprecated apiVersions.
entries.remediation: Pin action refs to a SHA or tagged release. Replace `:latest` and untagged images with explicit versions. Upgrade deprecated apiVersions to their current replacement.
entries.promotionPlan: Promotes to stable once the calibration corpus confirms regression-PR lift ≥ 1.5x on config-only PRs.

entries.title: AI/ML Surface Without Eval Coverage
entries.description: An AI/ML surface (prompt, agent, tool definition, model context, or model artifact) has no eval scenario covering it. Across 2000 OSS AI/ML repos, 136 of every 137 detected surfaces have this gap — the dominant AI-testing failure mode.
entries.remediation: Add an eval scenario (promptfoo / DeepEval / Ragas / framework-specific) that exercises this surface. Use `terrain ai list` to see other uncovered surfaces in the same repo and batch-fix.
entries.promotionPlan: Promotes to stable once AI-corpus harvest (re-clone in flight, 2026-05-12) confirms regression-PR lift ≥ 1.5× with CI lower bound > 1.0 on the 558-repo AI corpus.

entries.title: Framework Migration Opportunity
entries.description: The repository or package appears suitable for migration to a target framework.
entries.remediation: Evaluate candidates with `terrain migration readiness` and plan staged framework_migration.

entries.title: Migration Blocker
entries.description: Detected patterns will complicate framework framework_migration.
entries.remediation: Address blockers incrementally before broad migration changes.
entries.promotionPlan: Refresh trigger set to mocha→jest, jasmine→jest, unittest→pytest after confirming base rate ≥5 per 100 repos. Drop enzyme sub-rule entirely; AI corpus has zero enzyme migrations remaining.

entries.title: Deprecated Test Pattern
entries.description: Deprecated test patterns increase migration and maintenance risk.
entries.remediation: Replace deprecated APIs with supported alternatives.
entries.promotionPlan: Drop enzyme sub-rule; refresh trigger set; setTimeout sub-rule needs A3 scope gate (jest.setTimeout config vs bare setTimeout in test body). Path-role gate to exclude fuzzer/fixture/comment-only matches.

entries.title: Dynamic Test Generation
entries.description: Dynamic test generation may reduce migration and analysis confidence.
entries.remediation: Prefer explicit, static test declarations for critical paths.

entries.title: Custom Matcher Risk
entries.description: Custom matcher behavior can be difficult to migrate safely.
entries.remediation: Audit matcher semantics and provide migration-safe equivalents.

entries.title: Unsupported Setup
entries.description: Setup/teardown patterns may not port cleanly to target frameworks.
entries.remediation: Refactor setup boundaries toward framework-agnostic patterns.

entries.title: Policy Violation
entries.description: Repository state violates configured Terrain policy rules.
entries.remediation: Resolve violations or intentionally update policy thresholds.

entries.title: Legacy Framework Usage
entries.description: Legacy framework usage remains where policy discourages it.
entries.remediation: Plan and execute incremental migration away from legacy frameworks.

entries.title: Skipped Tests In CI
entries.description: Skipped tests are present where CI policy disallows them.
entries.remediation: Investigate skip conditions and re-enable tests or replace with targeted alternatives.

entries.title: Runtime Budget Exceeded
entries.description: Observed runtimes exceed configured policy budget.
entries.remediation: Reduce runtime hotspots or adjust policy to reflect intentional tradeoffs.

entries.title: Uncovered AI Surface
entries.description: AI surfaces (prompts, tools, datasets) have zero test or scenario coverage.
entries.remediation: Add eval scenarios that exercise this AI surface — untested prompts and tools can change behavior silently.
entries.promotionPlan: Coverage attribution depends on .terrain/terrain.yaml scenario declarations; precision/recall calibrated in 0.2 against the AI fixture corpus.

entries.title: Phantom Eval Scenario
entries.description: Eval scenarios claim to validate AI surfaces but have no import-graph path to those surfaces — typically caused by a prompt/surface rename that wasn't propagated to the eval YAML.
entries.remediation: Verify the test file actually imports and exercises the target code, or correct the surface mapping.
entries.promotionPlan: Promoted to Stable 2026-05-12 via Track 3 hand-validation against documented promptfoo rename incidents. Tier: Observability — silent eval-coverage gap, not gate-relevant. Severity raised from Medium → High because the failure mode (eval reports passing while running zero tests) is severe.

entries.title: Untested Prompt Flow
entries.description: A prompt flows through multiple source files via imports with zero test coverage at any point in the chain.
entries.remediation: Add integration tests at the prompt's consumption points to catch behavioral regressions.
entries.promotionPlan: Detection currently misses prompt flows that go through framework abstractions (LangChain runnables, LlamaIndex query engines). 0.2 ships AST-based prompt-flow tracing; promote once recall measures >=0.8 on the AI fixture corpus.

entries.title: Blast-Radius Hotspot
entries.description: Source files where a change would impact an unusually large number of tests.
entries.remediation: Ensure high direct test coverage and consider adding contract tests at interface boundaries.

entries.title: Fixture Fragility Hotspot
entries.description: Fixtures depended on by many tests, where a single change cascades widely.
entries.remediation: Extract smaller, focused fixtures to reduce cascading test failures.

entries.title: Assertion-Free Import
entries.description: Test files import production code but contain zero assertions — exercising code without verifying behavior.
entries.remediation: Add assertions to validate behavior or remove tests that verify nothing.
entries.promotionPlan: Gate-tier requires: (1) A1 multi-dialect assertion oracle + path-role test gate, (2) cross-file inherited-assertion resolution, (3) framing test flip <15%.

entries.title: Capability Validation Gap
entries.description: Inferred AI capabilities have no eval scenarios validating them.
entries.remediation: Add eval scenarios that exercise this capability to ensure behavioral regression detection.
entries.promotionPlan: Capability inference is heuristic in 0.1.2; 0.2 introduces the AI taxonomy v2 with explicit capability tags so this signal can fire only on declared capabilities, eliminating false positives. Promote once precision >=0.8.

entries.title: Eval Failure
entries.description: An AI eval scenario reported a hard failure.
entries.remediation: Investigate the failing case in the eval framework's report and patch the prompt or guardrail.
entries.promotionPlan: Planned — generic per-case failure surfacing on top of airun eval ingestion. Today's per-case failures route through the specific aiHallucinationRate / aiCostRegression / aiRetrievalRegression detectors.

entries.title: Eval Regression
entries.description: An eval case's primary Score dropped from baseline to current past the configured threshold, OR the run's PrimaryMetric dropped across all matched cases. Identifies regressions before merge.
entries.remediation: Inspect the diff for prompt / model / retrieval changes that affect the regressing case(s). If intentional, update the baseline with `terrain ai record`.

entries.title: Accuracy Regression
entries.promotionPlan: Planned — accuracy axis regression detector. Per-case score data lands in EvalRuns via the airun adapters; detector wiring is the remaining work.

entries.title: Citation Missing
entries.promotionPlan: Planned — RAG-specific detectors.

entries.title: Retrieval Miss
entries.promotionPlan: Planned

entries.title: Answer Grounding Failure
entries.promotionPlan: Planned

entries.title: Tool Selection Error
entries.promotionPlan: Planned

entries.title: Schema Parse Failure
entries.promotionPlan: Planned — depends on airun adapters surfacing parse-error buckets distinct from assertion-failure buckets (currently lumped into Failures).

entries.title: Safety Failure
entries.promotionPlan: Planned — depends on a uniform safety-verdict field across Promptfoo / DeepEval / Ragas adapters. The structural counterpart (aiSafetyEvalMissing) shipped earlier.

entries.title: Hallucination Detected
entries.promotionPlan: Planned

entries.title: Latency Regression
entries.promotionPlan: Planned

entries.title: Cost Regression (umbrella)
entries.promotionPlan: Planned — generic cost-regression umbrella that absorbs the prompt-specific terrain/ai/cost-regression detector.

entries.title: Context Overflow Risk
entries.promotionPlan: Planned

entries.title: Wrong Source Selected
entries.promotionPlan: Planned

entries.title: Citation Mismatch
entries.promotionPlan: Planned

entries.title: Stale Source Risk
entries.promotionPlan: Planned

entries.title: Chunking Regression
entries.promotionPlan: Planned

entries.title: Reranker Regression
entries.promotionPlan: Planned

entries.title: Top-K Regression
entries.promotionPlan: Planned

entries.title: Tool Routing Error
entries.promotionPlan: Planned

entries.title: Tool Guardrail Violation
entries.promotionPlan: 0.2 — tools-without-sandbox detection.

entries.title: Tool Budget Exceeded
entries.promotionPlan: Planned

entries.title: Agent Fallback Triggered
entries.promotionPlan: Planned

entries.title: AI Safety Eval Missing
entries.description: Agent or prompt has no eval scenario covering the documented safety category (jailbreak, harm, leak).
entries.remediation: Add an eval scenario tagged with the missing safety category and re-run the gauntlet.

entries.title: Prompt Versioning
entries.description: Prompt-kind surface ships without a recognisable version marker (filename suffix, inline `version:` field, or `# version:` comment). Future content changes will silently drift; consumers can't detect the change.
entries.remediation: Add a `version:` field, a `_v<N>` filename suffix, or a `# version: ...` comment so downstream consumers can detect content drift.

entries.title: Prompt-Injection-Shaped Concatenation
entries.description: User-controlled input is concatenated into a prompt without escaping, system-prompt boundaries, or structured input boundaries.
entries.remediation: Use a prompt template with explicit user-content boundaries, or run user input through a sanitizer.
entries.promotionPlan: Ships heuristic regex detection today; promotes to stable when AST-precise taint-flow analysis lands.

entries.title: Hard-Coded API Key in AI Configuration
entries.description: API-key-shaped string appears in an eval YAML, prompt config, or agent definition.
entries.remediation: Move the secret to an environment variable or secrets store and reference it through the runner's secret-resolution path.

entries.title: Hard-Coded API Key — Literal Shape
entries.description: An API-key-shaped string literal (e.g. AKIA-prefix, sk-prefix, ghp_-prefix) appears in an eval, prompt, or agent definition file. The structural half of the cycle-1 aiHardcodedAPIKey detector — preserved at observability tier so the literal-shape capability stays available while the secret-scanner-coverage split lands.
entries.remediation: Move the secret to an environment variable or secrets store and reference it via the runner's secret-resolution path.
entries.promotionPlan: Promotes to stable once secret-scanner-coverage-degraded (the other half of this split) is wired into CI integration as the gate-tier counterpart.

entries.title: Secret-Scanner Coverage Degraded
entries.description: The repository configures or references AI surfaces that should be guarded by a secret scanner, but no secret-scanner CI integration (GitGuardian, GitHub secret scanning, gitleaks, trufflehog) is enabled or configured. Coverage-gap counterpart to the literal-shape detector.
entries.remediation: Enable a secret scanner in CI and document its coverage in the project README. Re-audit periodically.
entries.promotionPlan: Planned — pairs with the literal-shape detector to cover both the in-repo signal and the CI-integration gap.

entries.title: Destructive Tool Without Sandbox
entries.description: An agent tool definition can perform an irreversible operation (delete, drop, exec) without an explicit approval gate, sandbox, or dry-run mode.
entries.remediation: Wrap the tool in an approval gate or restrict its capability surface to a sandbox.

entries.title: Non-Deterministic Eval Configuration
entries.description: An LLM eval runs without temperature pinned to 0 or a deterministic seed, so re-runs produce noisy comparisons.
entries.remediation: Pin temperature: 0 and a seed in the eval config, or document the non-determinism budget.

entries.title: Model Pinned to Deprecated or Floating Tag
entries.description: Code references a model name that resolves to a deprecated version or a floating tag (e.g. `gpt-4`, `gpt-3.5-turbo`).
entries.remediation: Pin to a dated model variant or upgrade to a supported tier.

entries.title: Prompt Token-Cost Regression
entries.description: A prompt change increases the token count by more than 25% versus the recorded baseline.
entries.remediation: Investigate the change for unintended bloat; bump the baseline if the increase is intentional.

entries.title: Eval-Flagged Hallucination Share
entries.description: The eval framework's own hallucination metadata reports a share of cases above the project-configured threshold (default 5%). Terrain reads this from the framework output (Promptfoo / DeepEval / Ragas) — Terrain does not judge hallucinations directly.
entries.remediation: Investigate the underlying eval-flagged cases; tighten retrieval or grounding before merging. If you disagree with the eval framework's classification, fix the eval scenario or raise the threshold (with a documented justification).

entries.title: Few-Shot Contamination
entries.description: Few-shot examples in a prompt overlap verbatim with the inputs of eval scenarios that exercise that prompt, inflating reported scores.
entries.remediation: Hold out the contaminated examples from the prompt's few-shot block, or rewrite the eval input so it isn't a copy of an example. Re-run the eval after de-duplication.
entries.promotionPlan: Substring-overlap detector ships today; promotes to stable once the calibration corpus tunes the threshold and adds token-level n-gram + semantic-similarity passes.

entries.title: Embedding Model Swap Without Re-Evaluation
entries.description: A repository references an embedding model in source code without a retrieval-shaped eval scenario, so a future model swap will silently change retrieval quality.
entries.remediation: Add a retrieval eval scenario (Ragas, Promptfoo, or DeepEval) that exercises this surface so embedding swaps surface as a measurable regression.
entries.promotionPlan: Ships the static precondition (embedding referenced + no retrieval coverage) today. The cross-snapshot content-hash diff variant lands once snapshot fingerprints are recorded.

entries.title: Retrieval Quality Regression
entries.description: Context relevance, nDCG, or coverage dropped versus the recorded baseline.
entries.remediation: Investigate the regression; revert the offending change or re-tune retrieval before merging.

entries.title: Detector Panic
entries.description: A registered detector panicked during the run; safeDetect caught the panic and emitted this marker so the rest of the pipeline could continue.
entries.remediation: Re-run with --log-level=debug to capture the stack trace, then file an issue at https://github.com/pmclSF/terrain/issues with the detector ID and the input that triggered the panic.

entries.title: Detector Budget Exceeded
entries.description: A registered detector exceeded its wall-clock budget and was abandoned by the pipeline. The rest of the pipeline continued without that detector's signals.
entries.remediation: If the detector is legitimately slow on your repo, raise DetectorMeta.Budget for it. If it should be fast, the runaway suggests a quadratic-or-worse code path or a hung I/O — re-run with --log-level=debug.

entries.title: Detector Missing Input
entries.description: A registered detector requires inputs (runtime artifacts, baseline snapshot, or eval-framework results) that the current snapshot doesn't carry. The detector was skipped; the rest of the pipeline ran normally.
entries.remediation: The marker explanation lists the specific flag(s) to pass to `terrain analyze` to provide the missing inputs. If you don't need this detector's signals, leave the inputs absent — the marker is informational.

entries.title: Suppression Expired
entries.description: A `.terrain/suppressions.yaml` entry has passed its `expires` date and is no longer in effect. The underlying findings will fire again until the entry is renewed or removed.
entries.remediation: Edit `.terrain/suppressions.yaml`: extend the `expires` date if the suppression is still warranted, or remove the entry if the underlying issue is resolved.

entries.title: Floating Dependency Version
entries.description: A dependency is declared without a version pin (unpinned, range-only, or moving git/url reference). Subsequent installs may resolve to different versions, introducing non-determinism in test and eval runs.
entries.remediation: Pin the dependency to an exact version, commit a lockfile that records the resolved set, or use a content-addressed git SHA reference.

entries.title: Secrets in Prompt
entries.description: A prompt-classified file contains embedded credentials (OpenAI / Anthropic / GitHub / Slack / AWS keys, JWT, bearer tokens). Anyone with read access to the prompt has access to the credential.
entries.remediation: Rotate the leaked credential immediately, then move it to an environment variable or secret manager.
entries.promotionPlan: 0.2 — Go-native regex detector ships first; gitleaks library integration is the deeper followup for richer secret vocabulary.

entries.title: No Tests for Code Unit
entries.description: A code unit (exported function / method / class) exists in the codebase but no test in the snapshot's dependency graph covers it. Untested code reaches production undetected when changed.
entries.remediation: Add a test that imports the code unit and exercises its observable behavior. The rule defaults to exported symbols only; configure `include_private: true` to widen coverage.

entries.title: No Eval for AI Surface
entries.description: An AI-typed CodeSurface (prompt / context / dataset / tool / retrieval / agent / eval_definition / model) has no Eval that claims to cover it. Model behavior can shift in production without any eval surfacing the regression.
entries.remediation: Add an eval scenario that exercises the surface and asserts on its output / metric / shape.

entries.title: Model Fixture Unpinned
entries.description: A model-loading call (from_pretrained / torch.load / joblib.load / load_model / snapshot_download) uses a path or revision that isn't content-addressed. The underlying weights may change without a code edit, regressing eval scores silently.
entries.remediation: Pin the load to a commit SHA (revision="<sha>" for HuggingFace), a version-suffixed filename (model_v3.0.pt), or a .safetensors-with-checksum format.

entries.title: Eval Without Assertion
entries.description: An eval test function runs to completion without any assertion / score / metric call. The test cannot detect regressions because it accepts any model output.
entries.remediation: Add an assert / score check that fails when the eval output deviates from expectations.

entries.title: Missing Random Seed
entries.description: Stochastic library call (np.random / torch / random / tf.random) in an eval or training file without a preceding seed call. Run-to-run results vary, masking real regressions.
entries.remediation: Add a seed call at module scope or in a pytest fixture (np.random.seed(42), torch.manual_seed(42), or transformers.set_seed(42)).

entries.title: Missing Env Pinning
entries.description: An environment-variable read in eval / inference code lacks a default value. The same code produces different behavior depending on which environment runs it.
entries.remediation: Supply a default — os.environ.get(KEY, "<pinned-value>") — or fail fast with a clear error message when the variable is absent.

entries.title: PII in Eval Dataset
entries.description: An eval-directory file contains PII-shaped values (emails, phone numbers, SSNs, credit card numbers, IPv4 addresses). Eval datasets that retain production PII expose customer data to anyone with repo access.
entries.remediation: Replace PII in the eval dataset with synthetic equivalents (Faker, Mimesis, mockaroo) or apply a redaction pass before committing.

entries.title: Insecure Deserialization
entries.description: A call into an unsafe deserialization primitive (pickle.load, torch.load without weights_only=True, joblib.load, yaml.load without SafeLoader, dill.load, marshal.load) executes arbitrary code on untrusted input.
entries.remediation: Switch to a safe format (JSON, msgpack, safetensors, ONNX). When the primitive is unavoidable, declare the explicit safe option (weights_only=True for torch.load, Loader=SafeLoader for yaml.load).

entries.title: Missing Performance Test
entries.description: A latency-critical AI surface (prompt / retrieval / agent / model / handler / route) has no benchmark or load test exercising it. Latency or throughput regressions ship silently.
entries.remediation: Add a benchmark under benchmarks/ or perf/ that records P50 / P95 latency for the surface.

entries.title: Data Leakage Suspected
entries.description: Source-level patterns associated with train/test contamination: preprocessing (scaler/encoder fit) applied before the split, or random train/test split applied to time-series data.
entries.remediation: Move scaler/encoder fits to AFTER the split. For time-series, use TimeSeriesSplit or a manual time-based cutoff.

entries.title: Missing Train/Test Split
entries.description: A training call (.fit() / .train() / .partial_fit()) appears in a training file without any preceding split helper (train_test_split, KFold, TimeSeriesSplit, cross_val_score, etc.). The model is fit on the full dataset; evaluation against the same data measures memorization, not generalization.
entries.remediation: Split the dataset before training (sklearn.model_selection.train_test_split, KFold for general use, TimeSeriesSplit for temporal data).

entries.title: Baseline Not Set
entries.description: An EvalRun exists for the current PR but no baseline is recorded. Eval-regression detection is disabled until a baseline exists.
entries.remediation: Run `terrain ai record` on the current main-branch state to lock the baseline. Subsequent PRs will be compared against it.

entries.title: Eval Pass Rate Dropped
entries.description: The success / total ratio across eval cases dropped past the configured threshold from baseline to current. Distinct from eval-regression (continuous score deltas) — fires on discrete pass/fail count deltas.
entries.remediation: Inspect per-case eval-regression findings for cases that flipped from pass to fail. If intentional, update the baseline.

entries.title: Snapshot Mismatch
entries.description: An eval case's recorded output snapshot diverged from baseline to current. Catches behavior changes the scalar score may not surface.
entries.remediation: Inspect the diff for prompt / model / retrieval changes affecting the case. If the new output is correct, accept it via `terrain ai record`.

entries.title: Impacted Test Failed
entries.description: A test selected by impact analysis as relevant to the current change failed. The change broke something the test suite already protects.
entries.remediation: Reproduce locally with `terrain test --selector regression/test-failed`. Fix the failure or, if the test is stale, update it deliberately.

entries.title: Performance Regression
entries.description: An ML model's performance metric (accuracy / F1 / AUC / RMSE / etc.) regressed past the configured threshold from baseline to current. Same shape as eval-regression but applied to classical ML metrics rather than LLM rubric scores.
entries.remediation: Inspect the diff for training data / hyperparameter / feature changes. If the regression is intentional, update the baseline.

entries.title: Missing Coverage Baseline
entries.description: The repository has eval surfaces but no `.terrain/baselines/` directory exists. Eval regression detection is disabled at the coverage layer.
entries.remediation: Run `terrain ai record` to create the baseline directory. Commit `.terrain/baselines/latest.json` so subsequent PRs compare against it.

entries.title: No Integration Test
entries.description: A code unit reachable from a production entry point (handler / route) has no integration test exercising it through that entry point.
entries.remediation: Add an integration test that exercises the handler / route end-to-end. The unit test stays as a fast inner-loop check; the integration test ensures the cross-stack contract holds.

entries.title: No Data Validation
entries.description: A data pipeline file (ETL / dbt model / training data loader) has no data-validation library import (Great Expectations, pandera, dbt-expectations, soda).
entries.remediation: Add data validation (GE expectations, pandera schemas, dbt-expectations tests) on the pipeline's output. Run the validation in CI on a fixed sample.

entries.title: Prompt Bloat
entries.promotionPlan: 0.2 — fires when prompt token count exceeds configured budget. Calibrated against dogfood corpus before promotion.

entries.title: Prompt Without Temperature
entries.promotionPlan: 0.2 — fires when LLM SDK call has no temperature= kwarg. Defaults differ across SDKs; explicit value is reproducibility-critical.

entries.title: Missing Prompt Validator
entries.promotionPlan: 0.2 — fires when prompt template has no instructor / guardrails / pydantic output schema.

entries.title: Prompt Version Skew
entries.promotionPlan: 0.2 — detects when same prompt template referenced by multiple eval scenarios under different version names.

entries.title: Retrieval Without Rerank
entries.promotionPlan: 0.2 — flags retrieval pipelines with top_k > 5 and no reranker.

entries.title: Cold Vector Store
entries.promotionPlan: 0.2 — fires when vector store is initialized but no index population call exists in the same module.

entries.title: Agent Loop Risk
entries.promotionPlan: Promoted to Stable 2026-05-12 via Track 3 hand-validation against public CrewAI agent-loop incidents (crewai #3441, #5102, #5891). Severity raised from Medium → High because the failure mode (unbounded API spend) is high-impact when it fires.

entries.title: Tool Without Budget
entries.promotionPlan: 0.2 — tool-call-enabled agent with no rate limit / cost ceiling configured.

entries.title: Target Leakage
entries.promotionPlan: 0.2 — feature column derived from target column (e.g., y_lag1 in features after target encoding).

entries.title: Duplicate Eval Rows
entries.promotionPlan: 0.2 — fires when eval dataset has >5% duplicate input rows.

entries.title: Schema Drift
entries.promotionPlan: 0.2 — fires when pipeline output schema changed between baseline and current run.

entries.title: Missing Eval Categories
entries.promotionPlan: 0.2 — fires when eval suite has happy_path coverage but no adversarial / edge_case categories.

entries.title: Orphaned Eval
entries.promotionPlan: 0.2 — fires when an Eval has no CoveredSurfaceIDs (references no surface).

entries.title: Cold Start Time
entries.promotionPlan: 0.2 — fires when first-request latency exceeds configured threshold (e.g., 2× P50).

entries.title: Token Cost Budget Exceeded
entries.promotionPlan: 0.2 — fires when per-run token cost exceeds configured ceiling.
